stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to 32-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Moscow and Kyiv signal a short Easter truce as diplomacy stalls and war pressures mount.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire: Not an off-ramp for the US but a life-saving ejection seat
stories.summary: A Middle Eastern country has for the first time single-handedly checked the war-making capabilities of the US and Israel

stories.headline: Stocks rise and oil prices gain as investors await ceasefire talks
stories.summary: Markets have remained positive, with oil prices edging slightly upward, coupled with gains for global equities ahead of the US-Iran talks in Islamabad on Saturday.

stories.headline: Trump Attacks Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly Over Iran War Criticism
stories.summary: In a lengthy social media post, the president attacked Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly and others in starkly personal terms. He also criticized the editorial board of The Wall Street Journal.

stories.summary: The president claimed he achieved them all. The reality is more complicated.

stories.summary: Iran’s parliament speaker warns that ‘time is running out’ as Israel continues to bombard Lebanon.

stories.headline: Lebanon reeling as Israeli strikes continue after 300 killed yesterday
stories.summary: Israeli air strikes hit Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon as rocket sirens blare in northern Israel, despite calls to extend a regional ceasefire.

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.summary: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.summary: Meeting in Beijing with Chinese president first of its kind in a decade

stories.headline: Xi tells Taiwan opposition leader people on both sides of strait are Chinese in rare meeting
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun’s visit to Beijing has sparked controversy in Taiwan, with critics accusing her of being too close to ChinaIn a rare meeting with Taiwan’s opposition leader, China’s president, Xi Jinping, declared that people on both sides of the Taiwan Strait were Chinese and wanted peace.The meeting in Beijing between Xi and Cheng Li-wun, the chair of Taiwan’s Kuomintang (KMT), is the first such contact in a decade. stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader calls for ‘reconciliation’ after meeting Xi
stories.summary: KMT leader Cheng Li-wun stressed shared cultural heritage and suggested she would slow Taiwan's military build-up.

stories.headline: Pakistan prepares to host US-Iran talks as ceasefire remains shaky
stories.summary: Islamabad is getting ready to host talks on Friday between the United States and Iran on a resolution to the war and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains at a standstill despite the ceasefire.

stories.headline: Nuclear Power Takes Another Tiny Step Forward in the US
stories.summary: New Jersey’s governor removes a moratorium on new reactors.

stories.headline: Ramey: Iran May Abandon Hezbollah for a US Deal
stories.summary: The stage is set for high-stakes peace talks in Islamabad, as US and Iranian officials work toward a fragile ceasefire. Adam Ramey, Associate Dean of Social Science and Associate Professor of Political Science at NYU Abu Dhabi spoke to Joumanna Bercetche on Horizons Middle East and Africa about what is on the table, and what it may mean for Iran's regional alliances (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bank Earnings Offer a Reset After Stocks’ Worst Quarter in Years
stories.summary: With war raging in the Middle East and private credit fears shaking markets, shares of Wall Street’s biggest banks are off to their worst start to a year since the regional banking crisis. But with the stocks now trading at relatively cheap valuations, strong earnings next week could spark a rally.

stories.headline: Ukrainians shot down Iran's drones in the Gulf — what does Kyiv get in return?
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president announced that Ukrainian experts in the Middle East have already shot down Iranian drones in “several countries” with domestically produced interceptors.

stories.headline: UK PM Keir Starmer visits Gulf to shore up ‘fragile’ US-Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: As UK PM Keir Starmer arrived in Doha as part of a Gulf tour he discussed efforts to secure the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: Trump issues NATO ultimatum to reopen Strait of Hormuz 'within days'
stories.summary: US President told NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte that NATO countries must work on a solution to resolve the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz immediately. Allies had originally committed to helping reopen the strait once hostilities had come to an end.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Vance heads to Pakistan for peace talks as Iran truce under strain

stories.headline: Petrol and diesel in Europe: Where are prices highest and lowest?
stories.summary: Petrol and diesel prices have risen significantly across Europe since the escalation of the Middle East crisis. Euronews Business takes a closer look at the latest fuel prices.

stories.headline: Retail Stock Traders Turn Into Sellers as Rally Spurs Skepticism
stories.summary: A bout of equities volatility fueled by surging energy prices amid the Iran war is eroding confidence among some of the market’s most reliable bulls: retail investors.

stories.headline: Gold May See Long-Term Rebound Despite War Setbacks, Banks Say
stories.summary: Gold is likely to rebound in the long-term even as the Middle East war upended the market, according to banks including ANZ Banking Group Ltd. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

stories.headline: Where Mail Voting Began, Worries Spread Over Trump’s Attacks
stories.summary: In the Pacific Northwest, mail-in ballots have been the norm for decades, but President Trump’s war on such voting has turned a point of regional pride into another partisan battle line.

stories.headline: Ares Plans Smaller Private Credit Fund After Record Predecessor
stories.summary: Ares Management Corp. is planning a significantly smaller flagship US direct lending fund than its previous record-breaking vehicle of $33.6 billion to speed up deployment of capital, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: Hungary Assets Rally Into Sunday’s Election on Orban-Exit Bets
stories.summary: Hungary’s election rally has pushed the country’s bonds and currency to the brink of multi-year highs ahead of Sunday’s ballot, which investors anticipate will end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year rule.

stories.headline: BofA’s Hartnett Sees the Surge in Commodities Lasting for Years
stories.summary: Investors should flock into commodities for the next few years as they benefit from global geopolitical and macroeconomic turmoil, according to Bank of America’s Michael Hartnett.

stories.headline: China Revamps ChiNext Rules to Lure More Startups to Market
stories.summary: China unveiled new rules for the Shenzhen stock exchange’s tech-focused ChiNext board, seeking to broaden listing options and make the market more attractive to fast‑growing companies.

stories.headline: Democrats Eying 2028 Presidential Runs Court Black Voters
stories.summary: At a gathering in New York, potential candidates made overtures to a vital Democratic constituency.

stories.headline: Djibouti votes as long-time leader seeks sixth presidential term
stories.summary: Politicians scrapped the presidential age limit last year, allowing Ismail Omar Guelleh to run yet again.

stories.headline: Riot police push back protesters demanding higher wages in Venezuela

stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: Ceasefire or no ceasefire, the Middle East's reshuffling is not yet done
stories.summary: Both sides have reason to end the war but share no common ground.

stories.headline: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: Stocks Pause With Focus on Weekend US-Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US stocks fluctuated after a seven-day rally as investors looked to talks between the US and Iran for signs a fragile truce can hold. Oil was set for its steepest weekly loss in nine months.

stories.headline: Saudi Says Attacks on Oil Assets Hit Output and Exports
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia said a series of attacks on its critical energy infrastructure disrupted oil and gas production and hit supply to global markets that are already reeling from the Iran war.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Student Hit by Projectile During ‘No Kings’ Protest Lost an Eye, Lawyer Says
stories.summary: The student, Tucker Collins, 18, was observing demonstrators in Los Angeles when he was struck, the lawyer said.

stories.headline: Melania Trump Says She Was Not Associated With Jeffrey Epstein
stories.summary: Responding to what she said were smears, the first lady said she never had knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse and was not a victim of his. She called for a congressional hearing for his victims.

stories.headline: Questions About N.C. Stabbing Suspect’s Mental Illness Loom Over Case
stories.summary: Decarlos Brown Jr., who is charged with murdering a Ukrainian immigrant, was found incapable of proceeding to state trial. A federal case against him will come first.

stories.headline: Ecuador hikes tariffs to 100-percent in feud with neighbour Colombia
stories.summary: Daniel Noboa has accused Colombia's Gustavo Petro of failing to take 'effective measures' against drug trafficking.

stories.headline: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
